coding.display: Other nonspecific abnormal finding of lung field

note.text: Incidentally discovered on chest X-ray following evaluation of rib fracture sustained in a bar altercation. Confirmed by follow-up CT scan. Planned surgical excision scheduled 12/12/2025 at Metro Health Center; surgery cancelled by anesthesiologist on day of procedure.
Stable. Managed with semaglutide (dose escalated to 1 mg weekly in October 2025).
Controlled on atenolol 50 mg daily (started March 2023).
eGFR 49 mL/min/1.73m² (reduced). Creatinine 1.8 mg/dL (elevated). Consistent with CKD stage 3 per labs drawn 2025-11-20.
Patient reports occasional bloating and heartburn.
Significant anxiety reported regarding upcoming surgical procedure. Alprazolam 0.25 mg PO q8h PRN prescribed at pre-op evaluation on 2025-11-27.
Initial dose for diabetes management. Escalated to 1 mg on 2025-10-15.

Dose escalated from 0.5 mg. Pre-op note (2025-11-27) instructed patient to continue current medications; semaglutide was NOT held before surgery on 2025-12-12. GLP-1 agonists typically require a 1-week hold prior to surgery due to gastroparesis/aspiration risk - this may have contributed to anesthesiologist cancelling the procedure.

dosageInstruction.text: 0.25 mg by mouth every 8 hours as needed for anxiety

coding.display: Comprehensive metabolic 2000 panel - Serum or Plasma

code.text: Pre-operative Laboratory Panel (CBC, CMP, HbA1c, Lipid Panel)

resource.conclusion: Pre-operative labs notable for elevated BUN (32), elevated creatinine (1.8, H), reduced eGFR (49, L) consistent with known CKD stage 3. HbA1c 5.9% mildly elevated. LDL 134 mg/dL elevated above target. Triglycerides 186 mg/dL mildly elevated. CBC within acceptable limits. Reviewed and signed by ordering provider.

reasonCode.text: Pre-operative medical evaluation prior to planned excision of left lung mass

qualitativeRisk.text: Low risk - RCRI score 0, estimated <1% risk of major cardiac complication

note.text: RCRI Score: 0. No history of ischemic heart disease, CHF, cerebrovascular disease, or insulin-dependent diabetes. Creatinine mildly elevated consistent with CKD but <2 mg/dL. Planned surgery classified as intermediate risk. ASA Physical Status: Class III (severe systemic disease - T2DM, HTN, CKD - but not life-threatening).

category.text: Patient safety incident - telephone encounter documentation

payload.contentString: Incoming telephone call from patient Michael Andrew Carter (MRN 45823917) on 2025-12-14 at 09:18 AM. Patient expressed significant anger and distress regarding cancellation of lung mass excision surgery scheduled 2025-12-12 at Metro Health Center. Patient arrived at Metro Health Center with wife and daughter, was taken to surgical area; anesthesiologist cancelled the procedure stating 'medications were not correct.' Patient expressed severe anxiety about cancer progression due to the delay. Patient stated he was planning to come to MoN Critical Access Hospital to 'find' Dr. Whitaker for answers, and stated 'someone has to pay for it.' Patient tone was angry and agitated. Hospital administration and hospital security notified due to patient's stated intent to confront the physician.

note.text: Documented by Rebecca L. Harmon, RN. Hospital administration and security notified per protocol.

Oxygen saturation in Arterial blood by Pulse oximetry

resource.conclusion: No acute abnormalities. Reviewed as part of pre-operative workup completed within one week of evaluation on 2025-11-27.

coding.display: Fracture of one rib, unspecified side, initial encounter for closed fracture

note.text: Sustained during a bar altercation. Chest X-ray ordered for this injury incidentally revealed a left lung mass, which was subsequently confirmed by CT scan. Specific rib and laterality not documented.

statusReason.text: Anesthesiologist determined patient medications were not appropriate for surgery on day of procedure (2025-12-12).

code.text: Surgical excision of left lung mass

dosageInstruction.text: Take 5 mg by mouth every 6 hours as needed for back pain
